task_completion.description: OpenICE interface is open with the Simulated Multiparameter Monitor and Vital Signs application running
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenICE application window is visible on the screen, specifically showing the 'Simulated Multiparameter Monitor' and the 'Vital Signs' clinical application.

task_completion.description: A full-resolution screenshot of the interface is saved to /home/ga/original_ui.png
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal command executing a screenshot utility (e.g., scrot, gnome-screenshot) saving to /home/ga/original_ui.png, or the file appearing in a file manager/terminal listing.

task_completion.description: A Python script is created at /home/ga/process_accessibility.py for image processing
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (like nano, vim, gedit) or terminal cat/echo command showing the contents of /home/ga/process_accessibility.py, which should include code for image manipulation.

task_completion.description: A grayscale version of the screenshot is generated at /home/ga/accessibility_proof_gray.png
task_completion.visual_evidence: The file /home/ga/accessibility_proof_gray.png is visible in a file manager, terminal listing, or opened in an image viewer, showing a black-and-white version of the UI.

task_completion.description: The mean luminance is recorded in /home/ga/luminance_report.txt
task_completion.visual_evidence: The file /home/ga/luminance_report.txt is visible in a file manager, or its contents are displayed in the terminal/editor showing a numeric mean luminance value (0-255).

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual OpenICE application
integrity.visual_evidence: The OpenICE application is genuinely running as a desktop application (visible window borders, taskbar presence), not just a static image viewed in a browser or image viewer.

integrity.description: The Python script contains actual image processing logic
integrity.visual_evidence: The script in process_accessibility.py uses an image processing library (like PIL/Pillow or cv2) to convert the image and calculate luminance, rather than hardcoding dummy files or values.

integrity.description: Outputs were generated by the script, not manually
integrity.visual_evidence: The grayscale image and luminance report text file are created as a result of running the Python script in the terminal, rather than being manually created by the agent using an image editor or typing into a text file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires creating four specific artifacts in /home/ga: (1) original_ui.png (screenshot of OpenICE Vital Signs), (2) process_accessibility.py (Python script using a library like Pillow to process the image), (3) accessibility_proof_gray.png (the grayscale output), and (4) luminance_report.txt (text file containing the calculated mean luminance value from 0-255).
